title: Verb 'to be' (am/is/are)
bridge: Guaraníme, ñande jaiporu 'che mbo'ehára' ha'éva 'yo profesor', ndaipóri verbo copulativo. Ingléspe, oĩ va'erã 'to be' opa árape. Péicha, ndaikatúi ja'e 'She happy', ja'e va'erã 'She is happy'.
rule: 'To be' oguereko mbohapy forma: 'am' (I ndive), 'is' (he, she, it ndive), ha 'are' (you, we, they ndive). Preguntas japo hag̃ua, jaiporu verbo sujeto rire: 'Are you...?', 'Is he...?'. Negativo japo hag̃ua, jambojoapy 'not': 'I am not', 'he is not' (he isn't), 'they are not' (they aren't).

common_error.explanation: Guaraníme, ja'e 'Ha'e vy'apá' verbo'ỹre. Ingléspe, 'to be' oĩ va'erã opa árape. Ani rejapo 'She happy', eiporu 'She is happy'.

examples.note: Jainverti sujeto ha verbo preguntas japo hag̃ua.

title: Personal Pronouns (I, you, he, she, it, we, they)
bridge: Guaraníme, pronombre personal oĩ verbo ñepyrũme (a- = che, re- = nde, o- = ha'e, ja- = ñande, pe- = peẽ, o- = ha'ekuéra). Ingléspe, pronombre oĩ va'erã opa árape, ndaikatúi jahecha'i. Avei, guaraníme ndaipóri género gramatical, upévare confunde 'he' ha 'she'.
rule: Pronombre personal inglés: I (che), you (nde/peẽ), he (ha'e kuimba'e), she (ha'e kuña), it (mba'e / mymba), we (ñande), they (ha'ekuéra). 'It' ojepuru mba'e, mymba ha temiandu rehe. 'You' ojepuru singular ha plural. Ani rehecha'i sujeto ingléspe.

common_error.explanation: Guaraníme, ja'e 'Ha'e che ryke'y' ha ikatu jahecha'i 'ha'e'. Ingléspe, pronombre oĩ va'erã opa árape. Eiporu 'He' o 'She'.

examples.translation: Ha'ekuéra che ru ha che sy.

examples.note: 'It' ojepuru mba'e, mymba ha temiandu rehe.

title: Articles (a/an, the)
bridge: Guaraníme ndaipóri artículo (a/an, the). Upévare, estudiantekuéra ojapyhy va'erã mba'éichapa ojeporu ingléspe. 'A' ha 'an' ojeporu peteĩ mba'e rehe (a dog = peteĩ jagua), 'the' ojeporu mba'e ojekuaáva térã oje'éva mboyve.
rule: Jeporu 'a' umi ñe'ẽ oñepyrũva konsonante ndive (a book, a car). Jeporu 'an' umi ñe'ẽ oñepyrũva vokal ndive (an apple, an hour – 'h' ndoje'e). Jeporu 'the' mba'e ojekuaáva térã oje'éva mboyve rehe. Ani jeporu 'the' temiandu guasu rehe (Love is important, ha'éva 'The love').

common_error.explanation: Guaraníme, ja'e 'Che oreko jagua' artículo'ỹre. Ingléspe, mba'e peteĩ rehe jaiporu va'erã 'a' o 'an'.

title: Regular and Irregular Plurals
bridge: Guaraníme, ñande jajapo plural -kuéra ndive, ha kóva ndojeiporúi opa árape (ojeporu año contexto oikuaa'ỹrõ). Ingléspe, plural ojeporu opa árape ha oĩ heta regla. Umi palabra ipu'akáva ojeporu -s (book → books), ha umi oñepyrũva -s, -sh, -ch, -x, -z ndive ojeporu -es (bus → buses, watch → watches). Avei, oĩ plural irregular (child → children, foot → feet) oñeñe'ẽmbaitéva.
rule: Regla plural regular: 1) Jeporu -s (cat → cats). 2) Jeporu -es umi palabra opáva -s, -sh, -ch, -x, -z ndive (box → boxes, dish → dishes). 3) Opa -y konsonante mboyve, ñambohéra -ies (city → cities). 4) Opa -f o -fe, ambue -ves (wolf → wolves, knife → knives). Plural irregular: child → children, foot → feet, tooth → teeth, man → men, woman → women, mouse → mice, sheep → sheep (ojogua), fish → fish (ojogua).

common_error.explanation: Guaraníme, mitã → mitãnguéra (regular). Ingléspe, 'child' ha'e irregular ha plural ha'e 'children'. Kóva peteĩ error común guaraní oñe'ẽvape.

title: Present Simple (I eat, you eat, he eats)
bridge: Guaraníme, ñande jajapo presente umi mba'e jajapóva opa árape (Che'u pizza opa ára). Ingléspe, oĩ avei presente simple, ha peteĩ mba'e tuicháva: 3ª persona (he, she, it) ojeporu -s verbo rire (he eats, she works). Avei, preguntas ha negativo japo hag̃ua, jaiporu 'do/does', guaraníme ndaipóri ko'ã mba'e.
rule: Presente simple ojeporu verbo forma base-gui, ha 3ª persona singular (he, she, it) ojeporu -s (o -es umi verbo opáva -s, -sh, -ch, -x, -z). Preguntas ha negativo: 'do' (I, you, we, they) o 'does' (he, she, it) + verbo base (sin -s). Ej: 'I eat', 'He eats', 'Do you eat?', 'She doesn't eat'.

common_error.explanation: Guaraníme, 3ª persona ndaipóri terminación especial (ha'e'u). Ingléspe, ojeporu va'erã -s 3ª persona-pe. Kóva error común guaraní oñe'ẽvape.

examples.original: She reads a book every day.
examples.translation: Ha'e omoñe'ẽ kuatiañe'ẽ ára ha ára.

title: Adverbs of Frequency (always, sometimes, never)
bridge: Guaraníme, avance adverbio ojeporu avei opa rire: 'Che'u arro siempre'. Ingléspe, ko'ã adverbio ojeporu verbo principal mboyve (I always eat rice), ha 'to be' rire (I am always tired). Guaraníme ikatu jaiporu opa rire, ingléspe ndaipóri upéicha.
rule: Adverbio frecuencia: always (siempre), usually (normalmente), often (a menudo), sometimes (a veces), rarely (raramente), never (nunca). Ojeporu verbo principal mboyve (I always go to school), ha 'to be' rire (I am always happy). Preguntas-pe, ojeporu sujeto rire (Do you always eat breakfast?).

concept_examples.most_common_01: I always drink coffee in the morning.
concept_examples.phonetic_01: /aɪ ˈɔːlweɪz drɪŋk ˈkɒfi ɪn ðə ˈmɔːnɪŋ/
concept_examples.most_common_03: They sometimes go to the cinema.

common_error.explanation: Guaraníme, ja'e 'Che'u arro siempre' (adverbio opa rire). Ingléspe, adverbio ojeporu verbo mboyve. Kóva error común guaraní oñe'ẽvape.

examples.original: I always get up at 7 am.
examples.phonetic: /aɪ ˈɔːlweɪz ɡet ʌp æt ˈsevən æm/

title: Have got / Has got
bridge: Guaraníme, ñande jaiporu 'ereko' (tener) posesión rehe. Ingléspe, oĩ mokõi forma: 'have' (I have a car) ha 'have got' (I have got a car). 'Have got' ojepuruve Inglaterra-pe ha ñe'ẽnga guive. Kóva ndoiporúi 'do/does' preguntas ha negativo-pe, eiporu 'have' directamente: 'Have you got a car?' ha 'I haven't got a car'. Guaraní oñe'ẽvape, hasy jeporu 'got' ha oheja 'have' añoite, ha upéi ombohéra preguntas 'I have a car?'.
rule: Have got / Has got ojepuru posesión, relación, característica física ha necesidad rehe. Afirmativo: I/you/we/they have got, he/she/it has got. Negativo: I/you/we/they have not got (haven't got), he/she/it has not got (hasn't got). Preguntas: Have I/you/we/they got? Has he/she/it got? Ndoiporúi 'do/does'. Respuesta: Yes, I have / No, I haven't. Ojepuruve presente-pe; otro tiempo-pe, eiporu 'have' añoite.

concept_examples.most_common_01: I have got a new phone.

common_error.explanation: Guaraníme, 'ereko' ha'e 'have' / 'have got'. Péro 'have got' ojeporu 'got' (participio), ndoiporúi 'get' (infinitivo). Ja'e 'I have get' ha'e error común, ja'e va'erã 'I have got' o 'I have a car'.

examples.original: We have got a big house.

title: Prepositions of Place (in, on, under, next to)
bridge: Guaraníme, ñande jaiporu '-pe' mba'e oĩháme (ógape, mesa ári). Ingléspe, oĩ heta preposición (in, on, under, next to) ha ojeporu mba'éichapa oĩ. 'In' ojeporu ambuéva apytépe (in the room), 'on' ojeporu ári (on the table), 'under' ojeporu yvy gotyo (under the bed), 'next to' ojeporu ykére (next to the door). Guaraní oñe'ẽvape, hasy jeporu 'in' opaite rehe, ha 'ag̃uĩ' he'ise 'near' ha 'next to' avei.
rule: Preposición lugar: in (ambuéva apytépe), on (ári, superficie), under (yvy gotyo), next to (ykére). Avei: behind (kuarepoti), in front of (tenonde), between (mokõi apytépe). Transporte rehe: on the bus, on the train, péro in the car. Preguntas: 'Where is the cat?' → 'It is under the table.'

concept_examples.most_common_01: The book is on the table.
concept_examples.most_common_03: The cat is under the bed.
concept_examples.most_common_04: The supermarket is next to the bank.
concept_examples.phonetic_04: /ðə ˈsuːpəmɑːkɪt ɪz nekst tə ðə bæŋk/

common_error.wrong: The book is in the table.
common_error.correct: The book is on the table.
common_error.explanation: Guaraníme, jaiporu '-pe' opaite rehe, ndajakuaái mba'éichapa oĩ. Ingléspe, ojeporu 'in' ambuéva apytépe ha 'on' ári. Ja'e 'in the table' he'ise 'mesa apytépe' – ndaipóri upéicha, ja'e va'erã 'on the table'.

examples.original: The milk is in the fridge.
The keys are on the table.
My shoes are under the chair.
examples.translation: Che sapatu oĩ silla yvy gotyo.

examples.original: The school is next to the church.
examples.phonetic: /ðə skuːl ɪz nekst tə ðə tʃɜːtʃ/

title: Demonstratives (this, that, these, those)
bridge: Guaraníme, ñande jaiporu 'ko' (ag̃ui), 'upe' (mombyry) ha 'peteĩ' (indefinido). Péro ndojekuaái plural demostrativo-pe. Ingléspe, oĩ mokõi distancia: 'this' (singular) ha 'these' (plural) ag̃ui; 'that' (singular) ha 'those' (plural) mombyry. Guaraní oñe'ẽvape, hasy jeporu plural, ha ojeporu 'that' ag̃ui avei.
rule: Jeporu 'this' mba'e térã persona ag̃ui (singular). Jeporu 'these' mba'e térã persona ag̃ui (plural). Jeporu 'that' mba'e térã persona mombyry (singular). Jeporu 'those' mba'e térã persona mombyry (plural). Avei ojeporu presentación-pe: 'This is my friend' (oĩramo) ha 'That is my house' (jahechaukáramo mombyry).

common_error.explanation: Guaraníme, 'ko' ojepuru singular ha plural (ko yva, ko yvakuéra). Ingléspe, 'this' ha'e singular ha 'these' plural. Kóva error común guaraní oñe'ẽvape.

title: Can (ability and permission)
bridge: Guaraníme, capacidad jajapo hag̃ua jaiporu '-kuaa' verbo rire (aikuaa = che ikatu). Ingléspe, jaiporu 'can' verbo mboyve, ha kóva ndojekuaái 3ª persona-pe (he can, she can) ha ndoiporúi 'to' (I can swim, ha'éva 'I can to swim'). Preguntas ha negativo japo hag̃ua, ndaiporúi 'do/does', jaiporu 'can' sujeto mboyve (Can you swim?) ha 'not' (I cannot / can't).
rule: 'Can' ojepuru: 1) capacidad (I can play the piano), 2) permiso (Can I go to the bathroom?), 3) posibilidad (It can rain tomorrow). Ndojechangei opa persona-pe. Afirmativo: I/you/he/she/it/we/they can + verbo base. Negativo: cannot (can't) + verbo base. Preguntas: Can + sujeto + verbo base? Respuesta: Yes, I can / No, I can't.

common_error.explanation: Guaraníme, jaiporu '-kuaa' verbo rire (aikuaa = che ikatu). Ingléspe, 'can' ojeporu verbo base ndive, ndoiporúi 'to'. Kóva error común guaraní oñe'ẽvape ombohérava 'can to'.

examples.original: Can they come? Yes, they can.

title: Questions with 'wh-' (what, where, who, when)
bridge: Guaraníme, ñande japoru 'pa', 'piko' térã ñe'ẽnga pytũ porã pregunta japo hag̃ua, ndoiporúi auxiliar ha ndoinverti orden. Ingléspe, pregunta ojeporu 'wh-' palabra (what, where, who, when) + auxiliar (o 'to be') + sujeto + verbo principal. Ej: 'Where do you live?' (¿Mamo reiko?). Guaraní oñe'ẽvape, hasy jeporu 'do/does' ha avei jeporu 'not' verbo mboyve ('I not like pizza').
rule: Pregunta wh-: 1) 'to be' ndive: Wh-word + am/is/are + sujeto? (Where is the bank?). 2) 'can' ndive: Wh-word + can + sujeto + verbo? (What can you do?). 3) Presente simple (ambue verbo): Wh-word + do/does + sujeto + verbo base? (What do you eat?). 4) Pregunta indirecta (Can you tell me...): orden sujeto + verbo (sin inversión): 'Can you tell me where the station is?' (ha'éva 'where is the station').

common_error.explanation: Guaraníme, ja'e 'Mba'épa rehayhu?' ndoiporúi auxiliar. Ingléspe, pregunta japo hag̃ua jaiporu va'erã 'do/does'. Kóva error común guaraní oñe'ẽvape.

title: There is / There are
bridge: Guaraníme, ñande jaiporu 'oĩ' (hay/existe) mba'e oĩháme, ndoiporúi número (singular/plural). Ingléspe, oĩ mokõi forma: 'there is' (singular) ha 'there are' (plural), ojeporu mba'éichapa oĩ. Guaraní oñe'ẽvape, hasy jeporu 'there is' ha 'there are' oñondive, ha avei oheja 'there' (Two cats in the garden).
rule: Jeporu 'There is' sustantivo contable singular ha incontable rehe (There is some water). Jeporu 'There are' sustantivo contable plural rehe (There are three chairs). Preguntas: Is there...? / Are there...? Negativo: There isn't / There aren't. Avei, jeporu 'any' preguntas ha negativo-pe: 'Is there any milk?' ha 'There isn't any milk'.

concept_examples.most_common_01: There is a cat in the garden.
concept_examples.phonetic_01: /ðeər ɪz ə kæt ɪn ðə ˈɡɑːdən/
concept_examples.most_common_02: There are two chairs in the kitchen.
concept_examples.phonetic_02: /ðeər ɑːr tuː tʃeəz ɪn ðə ˈkɪtʃɪn/
concept_examples.most_common_03: Is there a bank near here?
concept_examples.most_common_04: There aren't any students in the class.
concept_examples.phonetic_04: /ðeər ɑːnt ˈeni ˈstjuːdənts ɪn ðə klɑːs/

common_error.explanation: Guaraníme, 'oĩ' ndoiporúi número (singular/plural). Ingléspe, 'there is' ojeporu singular ha 'there are' plural. Kóva error común guaraní oñe'ẽvape.

examples.original: There is a phone on the desk.
examples.phonetic: /ðeər ɪz ə fəʊn ɒn ðə desk/

examples.original: There are three cars in the street.
examples.phonetic: /ðeər ɑːr θriː kɑːrz ɪn ðə striːt/

levels.label: Questions with Is there / Are there
levels.translation: Preguntas Is there / Are there ndive

examples.original: Are there any eggs in the fridge?
examples.phonetic: /ɑːr ðeər ˈeni eɡz ɪn ðə frɪdʒ/

examples.original: There isn't any sugar in the cupboard.
examples.phonetic: /ðeər ˈɪzənt ˈeni ˈʃʊɡər ɪn ðə ˈkʌbəd/
